review_notes: The functions provided accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to access enforcement and network policy management. The naming conventions are mostly consistent with the provided standards. However, I recommend ensuring that the function names explicitly convey the security intent, such as emphasizing the prevention of unauthorized access to security-relevant information.
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | security - relevant information prevent access to [ assignm
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(5) focuses on access enforcement and preventing unauthorized access to security-relevant information. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that RBAC policies do not grant excessive privileges, such as cluster-admin roles, and that least privilege is enforced. Additionally, network policies should be configured to deny ingress and egress by default to prevent unauthorized network access.
